builder.name: RWA Protocol Builder
builder.subject: RWA20 — Open Standard That Your Platform Could Build On
builder.body:
Hi {{name}},

I'm the founder of RWA20 Protocol — an open-source Ethereum standard for tokenizing real-world assets with native multi-jurisdiction legal compliance.

I've been following {{org}}'s work on {{focus}} and think there's a natural alignment. RWA20 is designed to be what ERC20 is to fungible tokens — a permissionless standard that platforms like {{org}} can adopt, extend, or build on top of.

What makes RWA20 different:
• Compliance is embedded inside ERC20's _update() hook — impossible to bypass via DEX, bridge, or direct transfer
• Multi-jurisdiction OR-logic: 6 live jurisdictions (SG, CH, AE, LI, MY, US) with one-contract extensibility
• On-chain legal attestations via EAS + Kleros — traceable from token to court document
• Zero fees in bootstrap phase — MIT licensed, permissionless

We've shipped 75 Solidity contracts with 0 compilation errors, a full Next.js DApp, and CONTRIBUTING.md that makes adding a new jurisdiction as simple as one contract + one PR.

I'd love to explore:
1. Whether RWA20 could serve as the compliance standard underlying {{org}}'s token architecture
2. Collaboration on shared jurisdiction modules
3. A simple integration pilot

GitHub: https://github.com/lxd422152276/rwa20-protocol
Website: https://lxd422152276.github.io/rwa20-protocol/

Worth a 20-minute call?

Best,
[Your name]
RWA20 Protocol
lxd422152276@gmail.com

influencer.name: DeFi Thought Leader
influencer.subject: RWA20: The Missing Compliance Standard DeFi Needs to Absorb $500T in Real Assets
influencer.body:
Hi {{name}},

I'm reaching out because of your work on {{focus}} — specifically the challenge of making real-world assets composable with DeFi.

I've been building RWA20 Protocol: an open-source standard that embeds multi-jurisdiction legal compliance directly into ERC20 tokens. The key insight is that compliance has to be a protocol primitive — not an external check — otherwise it will always be bypassable.

The result: the first token standard where a Singapore Accredited Investor and a US Reg D investor can transact in the same token, with full legal compliance enforced on-chain, without either party needing separate accounts or legal wrappers.

We support 6 jurisdictions live today: SG · CH · AE · LI · MY · US — with one-contract extensibility for any new country.

75 contracts, MIT licensed, 0 fees in bootstrap phase.

I think this solves a real problem you've been thinking about. Would love your perspective — and if you find it compelling, any signal amplification would mean the world to an early-stage open-source project.

GitHub: https://github.com/lxd422152276/rwa20-protocol
Whitepaper: https://lxd422152276.github.io/rwa20-protocol/whitepaper.html

Even a quick "interesting" or "here's what I'd change" reply would be genuinely valuable.

Thanks,
[Your name]
RWA20 Protocol

legal.name: Legal & Compliance Professional
legal.subject: RWA20 Protocol — We Need Lawyers to Help Build the Legal Standard for RWA Tokenization
legal.body:
Hi {{name}},

I'm building RWA20 Protocol — an open-source smart contract protocol for tokenizing real-world assets — and I specifically need {{focus}} expertise to make it work.

Here's the challenge: we've built the technical infrastructure (75 Solidity contracts, 6 jurisdiction modules), but the quality of each jurisdiction module depends on deep legal knowledge that smart contract developers simply don't have.

We've built jurisdiction modules for: Singapore (MAS SFA §274/275), Switzerland (FINMA FinSA + DLT Act Registerwertrechte), UAE (ADGM FSRA + VARA), Liechtenstein (TVTG Token Container Model), Malaysia (SC CMSA), and United States (SEC Reg D 506(b/c) + Reg S).

What we need from legal experts like you:
1. Review of existing jurisdiction modules for legal accuracy
2. Contribution of new jurisdiction modules your firm specializes in
3. Attestor certification — becoming a trusted legal attestor in the on-chain registry
4. Co-authorship credit in CONTRIBUTING.md and the protocol documentation

This is genuinely novel: the first time legal compliance for securities has been encoded as open-source smart contract modules, reviewable and auditable by anyone.

Would you be open to a brief review of the {{org}}-relevant jurisdiction module? We'd love to co-author the legal accuracy documentation with your firm's name attached.

GitHub: https://github.com/lxd422152276/rwa20-protocol
Legal Architecture Docs: https://github.com/lxd422152276/rwa20-protocol/blob/main/docs/legal-compliance-architecture.md

Best regards,
[Your name]
RWA20 Protocol
lxd422152276@gmail.com

regulator.name: Regulator / Standards Body
regulator.subject: RWA20 Open Protocol — Seeking Regulatory Engagement for a Global RWA Tokenization Standard
regulator.body:
Dear {{name}} Team,

I am writing to introduce RWA20 Protocol and seek {{org}}'s engagement with what we believe may become an important infrastructure standard for real-world asset tokenization.

RWA20 is an open-source smart contract protocol (MIT licensed) that implements legal compliance requirements from multiple jurisdictions — including {{focus}} — directly within the token transfer mechanism. Key points for your consideration:

1. REGULATORY ACCURACY: We have implemented jurisdiction-specific rules (investor classification, transfer restrictions, holding periods, sanctions screening) as auditable open-source code. This means any regulator can inspect exactly how their rules are implemented.

2. ON-CHAIN LEGAL ATTESTATIONS: The protocol integrates with the Ethereum Attestation Service and supports property titles, regulatory approvals, KYC verification, and court orders as verifiable on-chain records.

3. EXTENSIBILITY: The architecture is designed so your jurisdiction's requirements can be implemented in a single smart contract module, independently auditable, and independently deployable.

4. OPEN STANDARD: As an MIT-licensed open protocol, RWA20 is not commercially driven. Our goal is standardization — similar to how HTTP standardized web communication.

We would be honored to:
• Present the protocol to your FinTech or Digital Assets team
• Receive official guidance on whether our implementation of {{focus}} is accurate
• Explore whether {{org}} would consider endorsing or engaging with the standard

GitHub: https://github.com/lxd422152276/rwa20-protocol
Technical Whitepaper: https://lxd422152276.github.io/rwa20-protocol/whitepaper.html

Thank you for your consideration.

Respectfully,
[Your name]
RWA20 Protocol
lxd422152276@gmail.com

vc.name: Venture Capital
vc.subject: RWA20 — Building the ERC20 Standard Moment for Real-World Assets
vc.body:
Hi {{name}},

Short version: I'm building RWA20 Protocol, and I think it has the potential to be the ERC20 of real-world assets — a permissionless standard that an entire ecosystem builds on top of.

Why this is the right timing:
• Blackrock launched BUIDL ($500M tokenized fund)
• MakerDAO holds $1B+ in RWA collateral
• Every major TradFi institution is piloting tokenization
• But there's NO open standard for compliance — every platform rebuilds it from scratch

What RWA20 is:
• 75 open-source Solidity contracts, MIT licensed, 0 compilation errors
• Compliance embedded in ERC20's _update() hook — impossible to bypass
• 6 live jurisdictions (SG, CH, AE, LI, MY, US) with one-contract extensibility
• On-chain legal attestations via EAS + Kleros + ADGM Courts
• Zero fees in bootstrap phase

Business model:
• Phase 0: Zero fees → maximum adoption
• Phase 1: 0.05% issuance fee → ~$125K ARR
• Phase 2: RWAG governance token → ~$1.5M ARR
• Phase 3: Enterprise SaaS + certified modules → $5–25M ARR

This is the infrastructure layer. The goal is not to be the biggest RWA platform — it's to be the standard that all platforms use.

I'd welcome a conversation to learn your perspective on the RWA infrastructure space, and share what we're building.

GitHub: https://github.com/lxd422152276/rwa20-protocol

Best,
[Your name]

institutional.name: Institutional Finance
institutional.subject: RWA20 Protocol — Open Infrastructure for Your Tokenization Initiative
institutional.body:
Dear {{name}},

I am the founder of RWA20 Protocol, an open-source smart contract standard for tokenizing real-world assets with native multi-jurisdiction legal compliance.

Given {{org}}'s work on {{focus}}, I wanted to introduce a piece of infrastructure that could be relevant to your tokenization roadmap.

RWA20 addresses the core operational challenge in institutional RWA tokenization: legal compliance across multiple jurisdictions, simultaneously, without rebuilding the compliance stack for each new market.

Key capabilities:
• Multi-jurisdiction compliance (SG, CH, AE, LI, MY, US) in a single token standard
• On-chain legal attestations — property titles, audit certificates, regulatory approvals verifiable on Ethereum
• Institutional investor classification (Professional Client, Qualified Investor, Accredited Investor) enforced at the protocol level
• Revenue distribution infrastructure for any yield-bearing asset

The protocol is MIT licensed and designed to be adopted as an industry standard. We are in early conversations with several RWA platforms and asset managers.

Would anyone at {{org}} be available for a brief conversation about your tokenization roadmap and where RWA20 might fit?

GitHub: https://github.com/lxd422152276/rwa20-protocol
Whitepaper: https://lxd422152276.github.io/rwa20-protocol/whitepaper.html

Thank you for your time.

Best regards,
[Your name]
RWA20 Protocol
lxd422152276@gmail.com

media.name: Journalist / Researcher
media.subject: Story Tip: The First Open Standard for RWA Tokenization Compliance
media.body:
Hi {{name}},

I have a story tip for you, given your coverage of {{focus}}.

A new open-source protocol called RWA20 was just published on GitHub — and I think it represents a meaningful moment for the RWA tokenization space.

The headline: For the first time, legal compliance for real-world asset tokenization — including Singapore MAS, Swiss FINMA, US SEC Reg D, UAE ADGM, Liechtenstein TVTG, and Malaysia SC rules — has been encoded as open-source, permissionless smart contracts.

What makes it newsworthy:
1. Legal compliance embedded in the token itself — not an external layer, not bypassable
2. On-chain legal proofs via EAS (Ethereum Attestation Service) and Kleros court — first protocol to link tokens to court-verifiable documents
3. Designed explicitly as a standard, not a platform — MIT licensed, no fees in bootstrap phase
4. 6 live jurisdictions, extensible to any country with one contract deployment

The parallel: This is potentially the "ERC20 moment" for real-world assets — the standardization event that unlocks composable, global RWA markets.

75 contracts, 0 compilation errors, full DApp.

GitHub: https://github.com/lxd422152276/rwa20-protocol
Website: https://lxd422152276.github.io/rwa20-protocol/
Whitepaper: https://lxd422152276.github.io/rwa20-protocol/whitepaper.html

Happy to do a technical briefing, connect with the dev team, or provide additional background.

Best,
[Your name]
RWA20 Protocol
lxd422152276@gmail.com
